note: tmgmt_file is the TMGMT 'file' translator: export a job to XLIFF/HTML and import the translation back; it defines a pluggable Format sub-type. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I want to send TMGMT content to an outside agency as a file and import their translation back. Which translator plugin id / submodule provides this?
evals.expected_output: The 'file' translator plugin (FileTranslator) from tmgmt_file: submitting exports a file, uploading the translated file imports it back.

evals.prompt: Which file formats can tmgmt_file export/import out of the box, and how do I add a new one?
evals.expected_output: Ships xlf (XLIFF) and html formats; add a new one by implementing a FormatPlugin (FormatInterface) in Plugin/tmgmt_file/Format, discovered by plugin.manager.tmgmt_file.format.

evals.prompt: What is the default export format for the tmgmt_file provider, and which setting lets the requester pick a different format per job?
evals.expected_output: Default export_format is 'xlf'; allow_override (default true) lets the requester choose the format per job. Settings live in tmgmt.translator.settings.file.

evals.prompt: Which tmgmt_file provider setting controls where exported translation files are stored (which stream wrapper), and what is its default?
evals.expected_output: The 'scheme' setting sets the stream wrapper (default 'public'), e.g. change to private:// for restricted files.

evals.prompt: Does tmgmt_file offer a way to script export/import from the command line?
evals.expected_output: Yes — it registers Drush commands (tmgmt_file.commands / TmgmtFileCommands) for scripting export/import.
